meanings.definition: give moral or psychological support, aid, or courage to
examples: She supported him during the illness
Her children always backed her up

meanings.definition: move backwards from a certain position
examples: The bully had to back down
Can you back up your claims?

meanings.definition: make a copy of (a computer file) especially for storage in another place as a security copy
examples: You'd better back up these files!

meanings.definition: become or cause to become obstructed
examples: The leaves clog our drains in the Fall
The water pipe is backed up
